prose:
**Fukui Reactivity Indices for Phenol** — DFT, **B3LYP/def2-TZVP** (tier `standard`, density-fitted), PySCF, gas phase, neutral singlet ground state + cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2) and anion (charge −1, multiplicity 2) for fukui pair evaluation. Geometry: user-supplied `.xyz`, used as single-point input (not relaxed). Mulliken partial-charge scheme (PySCF). All three electronic states converged successfully; charge-conservation checks passed (sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000).

**Most electrophilic site:** Atom 3 (C, ortho position relative to OH), f⁺ = **2.1028** (extremely high electrophilic Fukui index — strongest site for nucleophilic attack). **Most nucleophilic site:** Atom 1 (O, hydroxyl oxygen), f⁻ = **0.1620**. Dual descriptor (ΔDD = f⁺ − f⁻) shows pronounced electrophilic character on ortho/para carbons (C3, C4, C7: ΔDD 2.03, 1.55, 1.18) and slight nucleophilic character on oxygen (ΔDD −0.42). This pattern reflects phenol's well-known propensity for electrophilic aromatic substitution ortho and para to the hydroxyl group.

Full tabulated results (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, ΔDD for all 13 atoms) are recorded in the engine output. Integrity check: trustworthy (status OK). No warnings reported — clean run.</prose>
<parameter name="value">2.1028
